Manpower Distribution by Category

Major Drivers of -8.90% Variance & Mitigation Actions
Key Drivers
  • CWP-02 / BAB Field (-13.75%) — Final hydrotest and HDPE activities delayed.
  • CWP-04 / BAB Industrial Building (-41.39%) — On hold due to commercial; IC in negotiations with Company and EPCM to resume project.
Mitigation Actions Taken
  • CWP-02 / BAB Field: Additional crews for HDPE liner provided, and manpower from BU HASA moved to BAB. Further manpower will be moved end of July after BU HASA civil activities completion.
  • CWP-04: Commercial discussions ongoing between IC, Company and EPCM to resolve hold and resume project execution.

🚚 Expediting Progress
Engineering
1- ICSS + SCADA + RTU Modification - BAB (ABB)

RTU Manufacturing
• PIM completed on 27-Apr-26; Priority list issued to vendor on 12-May-26
• Revised GAD Code-2 approved on 18-May-26
• Manufacturing clearance issued to vendor on 04-Jun-26
• Sub-Ordering summary & Un-priced PO shared by ABB on 09-Jun-26
• ABB shall perform the Heat Soak Test in accordance with AGES, as agreed during the weekly progress meeting on 25 June 2026.
• ABB will also submit cost impact for RTU panel shipment from sea to air and for conducting FAT for Lot-2 as the PO covers only one lot.
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
2- ICSS + SCADA + RTU - Buhasa (Schneider)

RTU Manufacturing
• Priority list issued to vendor on 13-May-26
• Revised GAD Code-2 approved on 28-May-26 - Revision B was submitted on 28-June-2026 and under review with ARCO/AON. As discussed in the weekly meeting held on 25-06-2026, the Vendor confirmed that there is no cost impact (Canopy, Blind Plug, Gland Plate details for Cable entry, Color Code confirmmation, Tag Plates, Ethernet Switch)
• PIM Held on: 08-06-2026 (As per AON Comm. Team Loting is not required)
• Manufacturing clearance under preparation.
• Sub Ordering details/Un priced PO will be shared by SE Dated: 30-06-2026.
• APBG & PBG under Vendor Review.
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Procurement
Almadar 226 No.s of Flanges - manufacuritng in Progress
- Lot 1 (82 No.s) IRN Issued - ETA 24 June 26 - Received
- Lot 2 (97 No.s) EXW 25 June 26 - ETA 15 July 26
- Lot 3 (47 No.s) EXW 20 July 26 - ETA 15 August 26

1. Prestige – VO2 Issued for 34 Tons - Fab. DWG. under progress with vendor based on the revised/optimize inputs given by ARCO Engr. (Delivery at site 6 Weeks from Approved GAD/DWGS)

2- Coating Status: Coating has been completed for a total coated quantity of 455 KM, with a remaining balance of Approx. 23 KM (Including VO5). Meeting with vendor Dated: 24-06-26

3. LV Panel Boards: Submission of a detailed list of pending materials along with supporting documentation to close the punch points with Cortum. The last delivery payment is on hold by ARCO to expedite the Pening mateails delivery (glands, reducres, cables), along with 10% of the document invoice.
Requested vendor to submit load calculation regarding for Cable sizing inline with pending punch points.
3.1. Light Poles: Inspection complete dated: 21-05-2026 - Punch Point Closeout in progress forcast date: 30th June 26.

4- MDG: FAT (Qty. 04)
Doc. Status: In progress
Bill On Hold: Original Agreement Valid: 9 Feb 2026, Addendum 1 Valied Until 30 April 2026 and ARCO is wating for Cummins sign and stamp copy.

System		ElectricalSCMS Modification - Buhasa--Siemens / Elmec
Buhasa		-YesSCMS TBE - Code 1 Rev. B (ARCO share TBT, Deviation & TQ-03 for vendor sign & stamp to achive Rev. 01 to achive end of cycle.
SCMS TQ - Round 3
Pricing Schedule Share with Vendor
BAFO Submission = 22-05-2026 - Extension till 03-06-2026
Initial technical offer was submitted by Siemens. However, during the commercial stage, the vendor delayed providing concurrence on the EPCM GTCs, indicating that approximately 8 months would be required by Siemens’ legal team to review and finalize the same. Subsequently, a meeting was held between ARCO, Siemens, and Elmec, during which Elmec confirmed its willingness to provide the services on behalf of Siemens. Accordingly, all technical submissions, including TQs and deviations, will now be submitted by Elmec following their reassessment.
